comments.text: For what it's worth, if $50 \le s_n \le 10^7$, then $s_{n+1} - s_n < \log s_n$. Furthermore, $s_{n+1} - s_n < \frac{1.74 \log s_n}{\log \log s_n}$ holds for all $s_n \le 10^7$ as well, and note that $\frac{\pi^2}{6} \approx 1.64$. I am not sure how much stock one should put in this data, but okay. By the way, under the assumption of the ABC-conjecture, the upper bound $s_{n+1} - s_n \ll_{\epsilon} s_n^{\epsilon}$ was proven by Andrew Granville (ABC allows us to count squarefrees, Internat. Math. Res. Notices 19 (1998), 991-1009), which can be found here. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
